title: Smoky Jamaican Grill Tomatoes
description: A smoky and flavorful Jamaican dish featuring chopped tomatoes, perfect for meal prep and suitable for Buddhist temple-friendly cooking.
instructions: Preheat an iron teppan grill or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the grill, swirl to coat.
Add the cherry tomatoes, brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper to the grill.
Cook for 3-4 minutes, or until the tomatoes start to release their juices.
Transfer the tomatoes to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the grill, swirl to coat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the ice cubes to the grill and stir to combine with the spices.
Use a spatula to stir the mixture and help break down the ice.
Return the tomatoes to the grill and stir to combine with the spice mixture.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the tomatoes are tender and slightly charred.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.
Allow the dish to cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for meal prep.
Prior to serving, squeeze a sliver of lemon juice over the top of the dish for added flavor.
